skill.name: linear-issues-write
skill.description: Create and update Linear issues from CLI commands that use a Linear API key.
skill.summary: Manage Linear issue creation and updates through documented CLI commands.
skill.author: Bind

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command, environment, filesystem, and network indicators. Review confirms most command and keyword matches are documentation examples or false positives, but the skill legitimately requires a Linear API key and performs write operations. Publish is acceptable with a warning about credential handling and Linear data changes.

medium_findings.title: API Key Required for Linear Write Operations
medium_findings.description: The skill requires LINEAR_API_KEY in a .env file or the environment, and the documented commands create and update Linear issues. This is legitimate functionality, but the token can grant write access to project data and must be protected.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The metadata and documentation explicitly require LINEAR_API_KEY and describe create/update commands. The risk is real credential-backed write access, but there is no evidence of malicious exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Commands Can Modify Linear Data
medium_findings.description: The documented bun commands run issue creation and update scripts with user-supplied titles, descriptions, statuses, assignees, priorities, labels, and project names. These commands are expected for this skill, but incorrect use can modify or overwrite Linear issue data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly documented as write operations. The scanned files do not include implementation code, so command injection or input validation cannot be fully assessed here.

low_findings.title: Command Execution Matches Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: Most external command findings refer to bash examples and option lists in SKILL.md. These lines document how to run the skill and are not executable code within the scanned files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious lines are inside command examples or documented arguments. No implementation code appears in the scanned files at those locations.

low_findings.title: Bun Runtime Link Is a Benign Network Reference
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL finding is a prerequisite link to the Bun runtime website. It is not a network request, webhook, or data transfer instruction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 15 is a Markdown hyperlink in a prerequisite list. There is no evidence that this line sends data or performs a network call.

low_findings.title: Keyword-Based Blocker Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The C2 and weak cryptography findings appear to match ordinary words or labels, such as the SOC2 label example and option descriptions. No prompt injection, command-and-control behavior, or cryptographic implementation was found in the scanned files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are descriptions, CLI option text, or benign examples. No code or instruction in those locations implements crypto or C2 behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Environment-Based Secret Access Requirement
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill requires a Linear API key from .env or the process environment. This is a normal integration pattern, but it increases exposure if the token is broad, logged, or committed.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The requirement is explicitly documented in both files. The risk depends on user token scope and handling, not on confirmed malicious behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Write Command Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented commands invoke bun scripts that create and update Linear issues. This is intended behavior, but it can alter external project-management records.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command names and surrounding headings confirm create and update behavior. The implementation files were not present in the scanned file list, so deeper code safety cannot be verified.

content.value_statement: Teams need a repeatable way to create and update Linear work items from an AI-assisted CLI workflow. This skill documents commands that use a Linear API key to manage issue fields, labels, projects, and assignments.
actual_capabilities: Documents a create-issue command that accepts title, team, description, assignee, priority, labels, and project options.
Documents an update-issue command that accepts issue ID, status, title, description, assignee, priority, labels, and project options.
Uses LINEAR_API_KEY from a git-root .env file or the process environment.
Supports JSON output for scripting through a documented --json flag.
Notes that team, user, and label names are resolved case-insensitively.
limitations: Requires Bun to be installed before commands can run.
Requires a valid Linear API key with permission to create or update issues.
The scanned package does not include the referenced command implementation files.
Write operations can change Linear data and should be reviewed before execution.

use_cases.title: Create Structured Bug Reports
use_cases.description: Convert a bug description into a Linear issue with priority, labels, team, and project fields.

use_cases.title: Update Sprint Work
use_cases.description: Move existing issues to a new status, change assignees, or adjust priority from a command workflow.

use_cases.title: Automate Triage Notes
use_cases.description: Apply labels and assignment updates after reviewing incoming support or QA reports.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Issue
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Linear issue for this bug. Use team Engineering, priority high, and add a clear title and short description.

prompt_templates.title: Add Labels and Assignee
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Linear issue from this feature request. Assign it to the correct owner, add Feature and Backend labels, and return the command summary.

prompt_templates.title: Update Existing Issue Status
prompt_templates.prompt: Update issue ENG-123 to In Progress, set priority to medium, and add a concise description of the current blocker.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Batch Triage Commands
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these issue notes and propose Linear update commands for status, assignee, labels, and priority. Ask before running any write command.

output_examples.input: Create a bug issue for a broken login flow in the Engineering team.
output_examples.output: A Linear create command summary with title, team, priority, labels, and a short issue description ready for review.

output_examples.input: Move ENG-123 to Done and remove the assignee.
output_examples.output: A Linear update command summary that changes the status to Done and clears the assignee.

output_examples.input: Add Bug and Urgent labels to ENG-456 without replacing existing labels.
output_examples.output: A Linear update command summary that adds the requested labels while preserving existing labels.

best_practices: Use a Linear API key with the minimum permissions needed for issue writes.
Review generated commands before running them against production Linear workspaces.
Prefer specific titles, priorities, and labels so issue changes are easy to audit.
anti_patterns: Do not store LINEAR_API_KEY in tracked files or paste it into prompts.
Do not run bulk updates without confirming the affected issue IDs.
Do not use vague labels or priorities when creating operational work items.

faq.question: Does this skill create Linear issues?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents a create command for Linear issues with team, title, priority, labels, assignee, and project options.

faq.question: Can it update existing Linear issues?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents an update command for status, title, description, assignee, priority, labels, and project fields.

faq.question: What credentials are required?
faq.answer: It requires LINEAR_API_KEY in a git-root .env file or in the environment.

faq.question: Does the audit confirm the implementation code?
faq.answer: No. The scanned files contain metadata and documentation. The referenced script implementations were not included.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk warning because it uses an API key and performs Linear write operations.

faq.question: Does it support machine-readable output?
faq.answer: Yes. The documentation includes a --json option for machine-readable output.
